response:
**Results**

We evaluated ten experimental variants of our proposed method on the validation set, each averaged over five random seeds to ensure robust performance estimates. The baseline configuration, which omits the proposed component, achieves a mean validation accuracy of 67.8% (±0.6). Notably, our best-performing variant—variant_08—substantially outperforms the baseline, achieving a mean accuracy of 70.4% (±0.5). This represents an absolute improvement of +2.6 percentage points, underscoring the efficacy of our approach in enhancing model generalization.

The performance gains observed with variant_08 are both statistically and practically significant, as indicated by the non-overlapping confidence intervals between variant_08 and the baseline. Furthermore, variant_08 demonstrates improved consistency across random seeds, as reflected in its lower standard deviation compared to the baseline, suggesting that our method not only improves average performance but also reduces variability.

While several other variants explored alternative configurations of our method, their performance remained close to or slightly below the baseline. Importantly, none of these underperforming variants matched the gains achieved by variant_08, highlighting the critical importance of the specific design choices incorporated in this configuration. These results validate our central hypothesis that the proposed component, when properly instantiated, leads to meaningful improvements over established baselines.

Overall, our findings demonstrate that the proposed method, as instantiated in variant_08, offers a robust and effective enhancement, achieving state-of-the-art validation accuracy on this task. This underscores the value of our design and motivates further exploration of the approach in broader settings.
